annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.quotientKerEquivRange` gives the explicit isomorphism `G / ker Ï† â‰ƒ* range Ï†` for a group homomorphism `Ï†`.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.quotientInfEquivProdNormalQuotient` is the classical second isomorphism `H/(H âˆ© N) â‰ƒ* HN/N` for `N` normal.

annotations.label: Projective linear group via second isomorphism theorem

anchor.snippet: An application of the second isomorphism theorem identifies projective linear groups

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `PGL(n,R) := GL n R â§¸ Subgroup.center (GL n R)` directly but does not phrase its identification as an application of the second isomorphism theorem.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem and correspondence (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.quotientQuotientEquivQuotient` provides the canonical isomorphism `(G/N)/(M/N) â‰ƒ* G/M` when `N â‰¤ M`.

annotations.label: Correspondence/lattice theorem (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.comapMk'OrderIso` is the lattice isomorphism between subgroups of `G/N` and subgroups of `G` containing `N`.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (rings)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `RingHom.quotientKerEquivRange` gives `R/ker f â‰ƒ+* f.range` for a ring homomorphism `f`.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (rings)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `RingCon.comapQuotientEquivRange` is labelled as the second isomorphism theorem for rings via ring congruences, generalizing the classical `(S+I)/I â‰… S/(Sâˆ©I)` statement.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem and correspondence (rings)

anchor.snippet: Let R be a ring, and I an ideal of R

annotations.note: Mathlib's `DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotOfLE` is the third isomorphism theorem for rings: `(R/I)/(J/I) â‰ƒ+* R/J` when `I â‰¤ J`.

annotations.label: Correspondence theorem (rings)

anchor.snippet: is an inclusion -preserving bijection between the set of subrings

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Ideal.relIsoOfSurjective` gives the order isomorphism `Ideal S â‰ƒo {p : Ideal R // comap f âŠ¥ â‰¤ p}` for a surjective ring homomorphism.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (modules)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange` provides the canonical `M/ker f â‰ƒâ‚—[R] range f` for a linear map `f`.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (modules)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `LinearMap.quotientInfEquivSupQuotient` is the second isomorphism theorem for modules: `p / (p âŠ“ p') â‰ƒâ‚— (p âŠ” p') / p'`.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem and correspondence (modules)

anchor.snippet: Let M be a module, T a submodule of M

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Submodule.quotientQuotientEquivQuotient` provides `(M/S)/(T/S) â‰ƒâ‚—[R] M/T` when `S â‰¤ T`.

annotations.label: Correspondence theorem (modules)

anchor.snippet: There is a bijection between the submodules of

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Submodule.comapMkQRelIso` gives the order isomorphism `Submodule R (M â§¸ p) â‰ƒo Set.Ici p` between submodules of `M/p` and submodules of `M` containing `p`.

annotations.label: Congruence on an algebra
annotations.note: Mathlib has structure-specific congruences (`Con` for magmas/monoids, `AddCon`, `RingCon`) and `Setoid`, but no fully general universal-algebra congruence definition.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (universal algebra)
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the first isomorphism theorem for `Con`/`RingCon`/`Setoid` separately but lacks a single universal-algebra statement.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (universal algebra)
annotations.note: Only structure-specific versions (e.g. `Con.comapQuotientEquiv`, `RingCon.comapQuotientEquivRange`, `LinearMap.quotientInfEquivSupQuotient`) exist; no general universal-algebra version is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem (universal algebra)
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Setoid.quotientQuotientEquivQuotient` plus the `Con` and `RingCon` variants, but no statement at the level of universal algebra.

annotations.label: Correspondence theorem (universal algebra)

anchor.snippet: the set of all congruences on

annotations.note: Mathlib has correspondence/lattice theorems for subgroups, ideals, and submodules but no general bijection between congruences containing a given congruence and congruences on the quotient algebra.
